title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Francis Claude, the daughter of the Earl of Trentham, reflects on her marriage to Jeremy Claude, a lawyer who used his shrewdness to settle her father's bankruptcy. Although Francis has played the role of a loyal wife and mother to their son, Anthony, the marriage has long been characterized by reticence and a lack of verbal intimacy. Following Anthony's death during his military service, Francis became more emotionally hardened and efficient.

While drinking coffee in a room overlooking the garden of their Georgian-style house, Francis observes Jeremy exhibiting a characteristic habit of stroking his upper lip, a gesture she recognizes as an indication of inner turmoil. Although Jeremy initially denies any issues, he eventually admits to Francis that she has "made a bad deal." 

This admission triggers Francis's suspicion of financial instability, a fear rooted in her childhood experiences with her father's unpredictable economic status and the arrival of bailiffs. While Francis views money with cynicism, she understands that for Jeremy, money represents both security and identity. As Jeremy appears increasingly frail and sorrowful, Francis questions whether his company is failing or if the couple will be forced to sell their possessions.
